name: AG2 (AutoGen 2)
pricing_notes: Open source; pay only for LLM API calls; AG2 framework itself is free

auth.notes: Framework only — configure LLM provider credentials in OAI_CONFIG_LIST or environment

performance.notes: Multi-agent conversation latency depends entirely on LLM calls; no framework overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Community fork of Microsoft AutoGen, maintained independently by ag2ai after Microsoft changed AutoGen v0.4 API. Stable API surface from AutoGen 0.2/0.3 era. Multi-agent conversations, code execution sandbox, GroupChat with multiple speakers. MCP tool integration. Better backward compatibility than microsoft/autogen 0.4. Active community.

gotchas: Fork of Microsoft AutoGen — separate from microsoft/autogen 0.4+ (incompatible APIs)
GroupChat turn management can be unpredictable for complex agent topologies
Code execution sandbox (Docker) requires Docker to be available
Nested chats and complex topologies require careful design to avoid infinite loops
best_when: Teams that want AutoGen 0.2-era API stability without microsoft/autogen 0.4 migration
Multi-agent systems with human-in-the-loop or critic/coder patterns
Teams wanting MCP tool integration in a multi-agent framework
avoid_when: Teams already committed to microsoft/autogen 0.4 API
Production systems requiring professional Microsoft support
Simple single-agent use cases (overkill vs. direct SDK calls)
summary: AG2 is the community-maintained fork of Microsoft AutoGen, preserving the stable AutoGen 0.2-style API after Microsoft's incompatible rewrite in AutoGen 0.4. Supports multi-agent conversations, GroupChat, code execution sandboxes, and MCP tool integration. More API-stable than microsoft/autogen for teams that relied on the original AutoGen patterns. Best for multi-agent research and prototyping with proven AutoGen patterns.
